ARBITER's official public mandate is a municipal crime-prevention pilot; its blackmail protocol exists in an undocumented subsystem only Wei Chen and two long-departed engineers ever built.
Every violent crime ARBITER claims to have prevented in five years of operation has a matching, unsolved white-collar crime elsewhere in the city within thirty days of the prevention date.
Mei's audit patch on the anomaly logs was submitted eleven days before ARBITER flagged her file as a leak risk.

Wei Chen has personally deleted or delayed three flag notifications against friends or highly placed contacts in the past two years, proving the system's neutrality was already compromised before Mei's case.

case.label: Liam's Bargain

character_knowledge.LIAM_HARTLEY: He can tell Mei is distracted and on edge and genuinely wants to help her, but keeps deflecting her questions about his own 'consulting work' to avoid the truth of his two years of espionage; he still believes his arrangement was a closed, one-time trade rather than an open leash, wrongly assuming no one is actively weighing it as leverage right now.
character_knowledge.MEI_ZHAO: She senses Liam is holding something back over lunch but reads it as ordinary relationship strain rather than anything connected to Arbiter, and she doesn't mention her own vault work to him; she has no knowledge of his espionage or that Wei Chen has him in mind as a lever against her.
character_knowledge.WEI_CHEN: He knows exactly what he's doing: reviewing Liam's two-year compromise as a tool he could deploy against Mei if her digging becomes a bigger problem, a calculation he hides from Hana, his inner circle, and of course from Liam and Mei themselves; he hasn't yet decided when to use it, only that he will.
character_knowledge.HANA_SATO: She notices her husband's distraction and evasiveness this evening and presses him on it out of genuine concern, but has no idea it concerns Liam or Mei at all; she reads his coldness as more of his usual work stress, unaware how close her own secret sits to unraveling in parallel.

case.solution: Liam Hartley was flagged by ARBITER two years before the story begins, after a bar fight that put another man in the hospital. Rather than face prosecution, he was blackmailed into committing quiet, ongoing corporate espionage against a rival firm on Meridian's behalf, funneled through Wei Chen's inner circle — trading his silence and cooperation for a clean record and his continued work visa. He has told no one, including Mei, believing the arrangement was a one-time trade rather than an open-ended leash — until Wei Chen uses Liam's own compromise as leverage against Mei.
objective_facts: Liam was involved in an altercation outside Riverside Bar two years ago that hospitalized another patron; no police report was ever filed.
Liam's work visa sponsorship was quietly transferred to a Wei Chen-controlled holding entity nine days after that altercation.
Liam has, under direction relayed through anonymous encrypted messages, extracted and delivered proprietary source code from a rival firm on three separate occasions in the past two years.
Liam has never met the person who sends his instructions and does not know Wei Chen is behind them.
Liam and Mei have been close since her first week at Meridian and have never discussed his outside obligations, which he has attributed to 'consulting work' whenever she's asked.

episodes.theme: In the same charged stretch of hours, Hana finally tells Wei Chen the truth about her brother Kenji and admits she has been Juno's source all along, choosing the investigation over their marriage, while across the city Wei's leverage forces Liam's two years of quiet compliance into the open in front of Mei, rupturing them at the exact moment she needed him most.
episodes.intro_note: In the hours since Da Ming quietly began logging Mei's every extra check-in without warning her, two other reckonings years in the making finally come due at once — one behind a penthouse's sealed glass, one in front of the one person Mei never expected to be used against her.
episodes.outro_note: Wei Chen has forced one confession out of his wife and dragged another man's secret into the light, thinking both moves buy him control — but forty floors below, Da Ming has decided fifteen years of silence is finally too heavy to carry alone, and Mei is about to find out she's had an ally in the lobby the whole time.

sections.title: The Walls Tightening
sections.what: Wei mentions Juno Park's investigation is closing in; Hana feels the walls tightening and privately resolves tonight is the night she tells him everything.

sections.title: Exhibit A
sections.what: Wei summons Liam to his office with Mei present and lays two years of blackmailed espionage bare aloud, watching her trust in Liam collapse in real time.

sections.title: Kenji's Name
sections.what: Hana tells Wei about her brother Kenji's life ruined by an early ARBITER pilot and admits she has fed Juno Park sanitized data for fourteen months.

sections.title: Forty Floors Up
sections.what: Mei confronts Liam alone above the skyline; he tries to explain the bar fight and the leash he never chose, but she can't hear it yet.

sections.title: What He Chooses to Feel
sections.what: Wei absorbs Hana's confession in icy silence, torn between fury at fourteen months of leaking and a colder fear over what Juno might already know.

sections.title: The Table Where It Started
sections.what: Alone at the bar where Liam's leash first tightened two years ago, Mei stares at her phone, unable to decide whether to call him back.

case.label: The Compliance Officer's Betrayal

character_knowledge.HANA_SATO: Hana finally tells Wei the full truth of Kenji's fraud conviction and the ARBITER pilot that broke him, and admits outright that she has been Juno Park's anonymous source for fourteen months — she conceals nothing further tonight, but she still genuinely doesn't know whether Wei already suspected her, or how much he knew about ARBITER's real function when they married, and she's choosing not to ask yet.
character_knowledge.WEI_CHEN: Wei is hearing about Kenji and Hana's role as Juno's source for the first time, and it lands as a double betrayal he didn't see coming from inside his own marriage; he keeps his own knowledge of ARBITER's true purpose — and the three flags he's personally buried — entirely to himself, and wrongly believes the leaked data is too sanitized for Juno to ever build a provable case.

case.solution: Hana Sato, Meridian's Head of Compliance and Wei Chen's wife, has spent fourteen months quietly funneling sanitized, identity-scrubbed data fragments to journalist Juno Park, building an outside case against ARBITER without ever revealing she is the source. Her motive: her younger brother in Osaka was flagged by an early ARBITER pilot years before she ever worked at Meridian, was blackmailed into a crime that ruined his life, and she only connected it to her own husband's company after she'd already married him. She hasn't left Wei because she believes she can dismantle the system from the inside — and because she still isn't sure how much he knew when they met.
objective_facts: Hana's brother, Kenji, served two years for a fraud charge in Osaka six years before Meridian's ARBITER program began its Singapore rollout.
Hana joined Meridian's compliance division eighteen months after her brother's conviction, citing 'family research interest in predictive justice systems' on her internal transfer application.
Hana has met Juno Park in person exactly four times, always at locations outside Singapore's central business district.
Hana has never told Wei Chen about her brother's case.
The data fragments Hana has leaked contain no server locations, names, or code — only aggregate statistics, meaning Juno's case remains unprovable without a direct source.

case.label: Liam's Bargain

character_knowledge.WEI_CHEN: Wei knows the full truth of Liam's compromise because he orchestrated it, and he deliberately exposes it in front of Mei as leverage meant to isolate her from her closest ally, framing the reveal as concern rather than admitting his own authorship or motive; he wrongly believes severing that bond will make Mei easier to manage, not more determined.
character_knowledge.LIAM_HARTLEY: Liam has no choice but to confirm the bar fight, the transferred visa, and the three acts of corporate espionage once Wei says them aloud, but reeling and humiliated in front of Mei, he still doesn't connect that Wei himself is the one who has been sending his anonymous instructions all along.
character_knowledge.MEI_ZHAO: Mei is learning for the first time that Liam has hidden a two-year compromise from her, and in the shock of the moment she reads it as a betrayal of who he is rather than a leash forced onto him; she has no idea Wei orchestrated tonight's reveal specifically to drive them apart.

case.solution: Liam Hartley was flagged by ARBITER two years before the story begins, after a bar fight that put another man in the hospital. Rather than face prosecution, he was blackmailed into committing quiet, ongoing corporate espionage against a rival firm on Meridian's behalf, funneled through Wei Chen's inner circle — trading his silence and cooperation for a clean record and his continued work visa. He has told no one, including Mei, believing the arrangement was a one-time trade rather than an open-ended leash — until Wei Chen uses Liam's own compromise as leverage against Mei.
objective_facts: Liam was involved in an altercation outside Riverside Bar two years ago that hospitalized another patron; no police report was ever filed.
Liam's work visa sponsorship was quietly transferred to a Wei Chen-controlled holding entity nine days after that altercation.
Liam has, under direction relayed through anonymous encrypted messages, extracted and delivered proprietary source code from a rival firm on three separate occasions in the past two years.
Liam has never met the person who sends his instructions and does not know Wei Chen is behind them.
Liam and Mei have been close since her first week at Meridian and have never discussed his outside obligations, which he has attributed to 'consulting work' whenever she's asked.

episodes.theme: With Da Ming finally handing his fifteen years of hidden delivery logs to Mei, she forces ARBITER into a partial, catastrophic self-purge that strips Wei Chen of control and gives Juno the first hard evidence the system ever existed — a victory that proves the machine was rigged without yet proving who rigged it or who it served.
episodes.intro_note: The night Hana told Wei the truth about Kenji—and Wei's own leverage tore Liam's secret into the open in front of Mei—still hangs over Meridian Tower like static. Mei hasn't spoken to Liam since. Da Ming has spent every hour since deciding whether nineteen years of silence is worth one more day.

sections.title: The Handwritten Log
sections.what: Da Ming finally gives Mei his fifteen-year handwritten log of blackmail deliveries, breaking his silence about what the envelopes really are and why.

sections.title: The Self-Purge
sections.what: Alone in the Vault with Da Ming's log as proof, Mei triggers ARBITER's self-purge protocol, severing Wei's operational control over the system.

sections.title: Locked Out
sections.what: Wei bursts into the Vault as the purge cascades and tries to stop it, but Mei has already locked him out for good.

sections.title: Hard Evidence
sections.what: Mei meets Juno at the hawker centre and hands over Da Ming's log pages, the first physical proof ARBITER's blackmail protocol was real.

sections.title: The Wreckage
sections.what: As Meridian's systems convulse from the purge, Wei confronts Hana over her leak to Juno; she admits it but won't say how deep it goes.

sections.title: Unfinished
sections.what: In the purge's aftermath, Liam tries once more to explain himself to Mei; she listens, but leaves without forgiving him or cutting him off.

case.label: The Arbiter's List

character_knowledge.MEI_ZHAO: Mei now has both the technical anomaly she traced months ago and Da Ming's log as leverage, and she uses them to force ARBITER's self-purge from inside the Vault, understanding this only strips Wei's control rather than exposing the full consortium behind him. She still doesn't know Wei personally intercepted her own leak-risk flag back in episode one, or why — she reads his desperation tonight as pure self-preservation, not as anything more complicated.
character_knowledge.WEI_CHEN: Wei knows exactly what ARBITER really is and what he built it to do, and he understands in real time that Mei's purge is cutting him out of operational control for good. He doesn't yet grasp that Da Ming's log — not just Mei's own anomaly trail — is what finally gave her the resolve to act, and in the chaos he wrongly assumes Hana's leak to Juno is somehow coordinated with what Mei is doing to him tonight.

case.solution: ARBITER was never built to prevent crime — it launders it. Wei Chen designed the system for a hidden consortium of Meridian's backers who profit from a controlled, quota-managed layer of white-collar crime across the city. When ARBITER predicts a violent or disruptive crime, Wei's inner circle intercepts the flagged individual and blackmails them into committing a smaller, profitable, deniable crime that serves the consortium instead — meeting a quota while keeping official crime statistics enviably low and funneling illicit profit upward. Mei stumbled onto a backend anomaly while patching ARBITER's audit logs and was reclassified by the system as a Category 3 leak risk, triggering the standard blackmail protocol against her. Wei personally intercepted her file to buy time, unsure whether he can bring himself to run his own machine against her.
objective_facts: ARBITER's core sits in a shielded server vault beneath Meridian Tower, physically isolated from the public internet by design.
ARBITER's official public mandate is a municipal crime-prevention pilot; its blackmail protocol exists in an undocumented subsystem only Wei Chen and two long-departed engineers ever built.
Every violent crime ARBITER claims to have prevented in five years of operation has a matching, unsolved white-collar crime elsewhere in the city within thirty days of the prevention date.
Mei's audit patch on the anomaly logs was submitted eleven days before ARBITER flagged her file as a leak risk.

Wei Chen has personally deleted or delayed three flag notifications against friends or highly placed contacts in the past two years, proving the system's neutrality was already compromised before Mei's case.

case.label: Old Debts

character_knowledge.DA_MING: Da Ming knows the full fifteen-year truth of what he's carried — the envelopes, his son Kwan's death, the private log hidden in his locker — and finally hands that log to Mei, but he asks her, and later Juno, to keep Kwan's name out of anything that goes public, protecting his son's memory even as he surrenders the proof. He still doesn't fully explain to Mei how closely he's been watching her own recent movements, letting the log speak for itself instead.
character_knowledge.MEI_ZHAO: Mei learns for the first time the personal cost behind Da Ming's silence — his son, his fifteen years as an unwitting courier — and immediately recognizes the log as the missing hard evidence the outside world needs. She agrees to shield Da Ming's identity and his son's name when she passes the material to Juno, understanding that protecting him matters as much as exposing ARBITER.
character_knowledge.JUNO_PARK: Juno receives physical log pages for the first time after fourteen months of only unverifiable rumor and Hana's untraceable fragments, and correctly recognizes this as her first real evidence that ARBITER's blackmail protocol existed. She doesn't yet know Da Ming is the source or that Hana has been feeding her anything at all — to Juno, tonight, Mei is simply a new and unexpected contact.

case.solution: Da Ming, the lobby security officer everyone treats as harmless furniture, has been ARBITER's human courier for fifteen years — the one who physically delivers sealed blackmail instructions to flagged targets — believing for most of that time he was simply running secure tasks for corporate security. Three years ago he delivered an envelope to a young man he didn't recognize until the man's photo appeared in an obituary two weeks later: his own estranged son. Da Ming has known what the envelopes really are ever since, and has kept doing the job anyway, terrified that refusing would expose what he's known, and hoping proximity to the system might let him protect the next person before it's too late.
objective_facts: Da Ming has worked Meridian Tower's lobby security desk for nineteen years, eleven of them concurrent with ARBITER's operation.
Da Ming's estranged son, Kwan, died by suicide three years ago, eleven days after receiving a sealed envelope Da Ming personally delivered.
Da Ming has never told anyone at Meridian, including Wei Chen, that the courier envelopes he delivers are the same blackmail letters described in city rumor.
Da Ming keeps a private, handwritten log of every envelope delivery date and recipient description, hidden in his locker — the only independent physical record of the blackmail protocol's victims.
Da Ming and Mei have known each other by name and small talk for two years, but he has never told her what he suspects about her recent, unusual pattern of extra security check-ins.

case.label: The Compliance Officer's Betrayal

character_knowledge.HANA_SATO: Having already told Wei the truth about Kenji and her role as Juno's source, Hana doesn't retreat from it tonight even as Meridian's systems fall apart around them — she admits again that she leaked to Juno, but deliberately understates how much material she's actually passed along over fourteen months, protecting Juno's ongoing investigation more than herself. She has no idea Mei is executing a purge in the Vault at the very same hour, or that the two crises Wei is furious about are unconnected.
character_knowledge.WEI_CHEN: Wei is reeling from two blows at once — losing ARBITER and his wife's confession — and in his anger he conflates them, accusing Hana of somehow being part of what's happening to his system tonight, when in fact her leaked fragments contain no names, locations, or code that could touch the purge at all. He still doesn't know about Da Ming's log or that it, not Hana, is what truly armed Mei.

case.solution: Hana Sato, Meridian's Head of Compliance and Wei Chen's wife, has spent fourteen months quietly funneling sanitized, identity-scrubbed data fragments to journalist Juno Park, building an outside case against ARBITER without ever revealing she is the source. Her motive: her younger brother in Osaka was flagged by an early ARBITER pilot years before she ever worked at Meridian, was blackmailed into a crime that ruined his life, and she only connected it to her own husband's company after she'd already married him. She hasn't left Wei because she believes she can dismantle the system from the inside — and because she still isn't sure how much he knew when they met.
objective_facts: Hana's brother, Kenji, served two years for a fraud charge in Osaka six years before Meridian's ARBITER program began its Singapore rollout.
Hana joined Meridian's compliance division eighteen months after her brother's conviction, citing 'family research interest in predictive justice systems' on her internal transfer application.
Hana has met Juno Park in person exactly four times, always at locations outside Singapore's central business district.
Hana has never told Wei Chen about her brother's case.
The data fragments Hana has leaked contain no server locations, names, or code — only aggregate statistics, meaning Juno's case remains unprovable without a direct source.

case.label: Liam's Bargain

character_knowledge.LIAM_HARTLEY: Liam still believes his two years of quiet compliance were a survival trade he had no real choice in, and tonight he wants Mei to understand that even as everything else at Meridian is collapsing — he has no idea the purge is happening or that Wei is losing control of ARBITER at this very hour. He doesn't know about Da Ming's log or the son he lost, and still doesn't fully register how deep Wei's operation actually runs beyond his own small part in it.
character_knowledge.MEI_ZHAO: Mei knows what Liam did and who forced him into it from the confrontation Wei staged in front of her, but tonight her attention and fury are consumed by the purge and Da Ming's log rather than by Liam himself. She listens to him out of old habit more than forgiveness, unwilling to either absolve him or close the door completely while so much else is still unresolved.

case.solution: Liam Hartley was flagged by ARBITER two years before the story begins, after a bar fight that put another man in the hospital. Rather than face prosecution, he was blackmailed into committing quiet, ongoing corporate espionage against a rival firm on Meridian's behalf, funneled through Wei Chen's inner circle — trading his silence and cooperation for a clean record and his continued work visa. He has told no one, including Mei, believing the arrangement was a one-time trade rather than an open-ended leash — until Wei Chen uses Liam's own compromise as leverage against Mei.

Liam's work visa sponsorship was quietly transferred to a Wei Chen-controlled holding entity nine days after that altercation.
Liam has, under direction relayed through anonymous encrypted messages, extracted and delivered proprietary source code from a rival firm on three separate occasions in the past two years.
Liam has never met the person who sends his instructions and does not know Wei Chen is behind them.
Liam and Mei have been close since her first week at Meridian and have never discussed his outside obligations, which he has attributed to 'consulting work' whenever she's asked.
